_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to analyse the perspectives, beliefs, values, and attitudes of people and groups in the past, based on evidence from historical sources.

_source.w: We are learning to analyse the perspectives, beliefs, values, and attitudes of people and groups in the past, based on evidence from historical sources.
y: Perspective analysis reveals the human motivations behind historical events — without it, history is just a series of facts.
Evidence-based analysis of perspectives prevents us from projecting modern values onto the past.
Understanding perspectives builds the empathy and critical thinking skills essential for navigating a complex world.
eg: Analysing the perspectives of multiple groups involved in the same event — identifying agreements, conflicts, and silences.
Using primary sources to identify the beliefs and values that motivated historical actors.
Discussing how perspectives are shaped by identity — class, gender, ethnicity, religion, political ideology.
Examining how the perspectives of the powerful differed from those of the marginalised.
Evaluating whether our sources adequately represent the range of perspectives involved in an event.
Writing an analysis that explicitly connects perspectives to evidence and acknowledges the limitations of our interpretation.
